items.title: How Indie Artists Use Chartmetric to Promote Music
items.author: Hypebot Staff (sourced from Chartmetric's How Music Charts blog)
items.verbatim_quote: Chartmetric gives independent artists the same data and tools that major labels use.
items.why_relevant: Frames the Chartmetric dashboard as the parity-with-majors tool indie operators reach for — the exact positioning Sharon's catalog dashboard should claim.

items.title: Best Label Management Software for Record Labels — Compared & Ranked
items.author: Stevo
items.verbatim_quote: For most independent labels, the best starting stack is: LabelSpark for release marketing and fan capture, Reprtoir for catalog management and royalty accounting, and Mogul to audit your catalog for missing royalties.
items.why_relevant: Names the exact 3-tool stack indie labels run today — Sharon's dashboard should mirror these three jobs (marketing capture, catalog + royalty, missing-royalty audit) as core panels.

items.title: How Broken Metadata Affects the Music Industry (And What We Can Do About It)?
items.author: Dmitry Pastukhov
items.verbatim_quote: Keep track of the song's credits from the very moment there's more than one person involved with the project.
items.why_relevant: The single most cited power-user tip in catalog software circles — capture credits at session zero, not retroactively. Sharon's dashboard should default-prompt for credits at the earliest possible row.
